title: Office of the Taxpayers' Ombudsman - Privacy impact assessment summary - Canada.ca
content:
## Office of the Taxpayers' Ombudsman - Privacy impact assessment summary

## Office of the Taxpayers' Ombudsman Canada Revenue Agency

### Overview & PIA Initiation

#### Government institution
Canada Revenue Agency

#### Government official responsible for the PIA
Office of the Taxpayers’ Ombudsman

#### Head of the government institution or Delegate for section 10 of the Privacy Act
Access to Information and Privacy Directorate

#### Name of program or activity of the government institution
Taxpayers' Ombudsman

#### Description of the class of record and personal information bank
Standard or institution specific class of record:
Taxpayers' Ombudsman - CRA OTO 330
Standard or institution specific personal information bank:
Office of the Taxpayers' Ombudsman - CRA PPU 222

#### Legal authority for program or activity
Order in council P.C. 2007‑0828 :
Whereas, pursuant to paragraph 127.1(1)(c) of the Public Service Employment Act, the Governor in Council may appoint a special adviser to a minister;
And whereas the Governor in Council deems it necessary that there be a special adviser to the Minister of National Revenue acting as the ombudsman for taxpayers;
Therefore, Her Excellency the Governor General in Council, on the recommendation of the Prime Minister, hereby sets out in the annexed schedule the terms and conditions of employment of the special adviser to the Minister of National Revenue, to be known as the Taxpayers' Ombudsman, who may be appointed by the Governor in Council under paragraph 127.1(1)(c) of the Public Service Employment Act.
Section 4 of the Order in Council identifies the mandate of the Taxpayers’ Ombudsman to assist, advise and inform the Minister about any matter relating to services provided to a taxpayer by the CRA, including to review and address any request for review of a service complaint.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
